data.title: Hick's Law
data.description: Simplifies decision-making by reducing the number of choices presented to the user.

data.title: Fitt's Law
data.description: Design elements are placed strategically to minimize interaction time.

data.title: Miller's Law
data.description: Information is chunked into smaller, manageable units to prevent cognitive overload.

data.title: Aesthetic-Usability Effect
data.description: Focuses on creating visually appealing interfaces that enhance perceived usability.

data.title: Jakob's Law
data.description: Leverages user familiarity by aligning with common web patterns and expectations.
